words.definition: Excessive confidence or boldness; audacity.
usage: No one had the temerity to question his decision.
She had the temerity to blame others for her mistake.

words.definition: A countless or extremely great number.
usage: There are myriad stars in the sky.
A myriad of choices lay before him.

words.definition: Exceedingly idealistic; unrealistic and impractical.
usage: He had a quixotic desire to change the world overnight.
The plan was criticized for being quixotic.

words.definition: A person who can endure pain or hardship without showing their feelings or complaining.
usage: He remained stoic despite the bad news.
She took a stoic approach to her losses.

words.definition: Kept secret or done secretively, especially because illicit.
usage: They held clandestine meetings in the basement.
The clandestine operation was finally exposed.

words.definition: Very attentive to and concerned about accuracy and detail.
usage: He is fastidious about his appearance.
The editor was fastidious in checking every fact.

words.definition: A harsh discordant mixture of sounds.
usage: The city was a cacophony of horns and sirens.
The meeting devolved into a cacophony of shouting.

words.definition: Lasting for a very short time.
usage: The bloom of the cherry blossom is ephemeral.
Fame is often ephemeral.

words.definition: Able to withstand or recover quickly from difficult conditions.
usage: The community proved to be incredibly resilient after the storm.
She has a resilient spirit.

words.definition: A sound that is sweet and smooth, pleasing to hear.
usage: She has a mellifluous voice.
The mellifluous sound of the cello filled the room.

words.definition: To deliberately make something obscure or unclear.
usage: The politician tried to obfuscate the issue with jargon.
Don't try to obfuscate the truth.

words.definition: Tending to talk a great deal; talkative.
usage: The loquacious host kept the guests entertained.
He became loquacious after a few drinks.

words.definition: Having a ready insight into and understanding of things.
usage: The perspicacious analyst spotted the trend early.
She made a perspicacious remark about his character.

words.definition: Deceitful and untrustworthy.
usage: He was betrayed by a perfidious ally.
The perfidious nature of the plot was horrifying.

words.definition: Pompous or extravagant in language, style, or manner.
usage: He made a grandiloquent speech that said very little.
The writer's style was grandiloquent and overbearing.

words.definition: A person who acts obsequiously toward someone important in order to gain advantage.
usage: He was surrounded by sycophants who praised his every word.
Don't be such a sycophant.

words.definition: Present, appearing, or found everywhere.
usage: Smartphones are now ubiquitous.
The ubiquitous smell of coffee filled the air.

words.definition: Vehement or clamorous; expressing feelings in a loud and forceful way.
usage: He was a vociferous opponent of the new plan.
The crowd made vociferous demands for change.

words.definition: A person who is fanatical and uncompromising in pursuit of their religious, political, or other ideals.
usage: He was a religious zealot who refused to listen to reason.
She is a zealot for environmental protection.

words.definition: Lack of interest, enthusiasm, or concern.
usage: The campaign failed because of voter apathy.
There is growing apathy among the youth.

words.definition: Well-meaning and kindly.
usage: The benevolent old man donated his life savings to charity.
A benevolent smile spread across her face.

words.definition: Given to sudden and unaccountable changes of mood or behavior.
usage: The capricious weather made planning difficult.
His capricious decisions often frustrated his team.

words.definition: Humble submission and respect.
usage: He addressed her with great deference.
In deference to his wishes, we canceled the party.

words.definition: A person or thing that is mysterious, puzzling, or difficult to understand.
usage: He remains an enigma to his colleagues.
The strange disappearance is an enigma.

words.definition: Fond of company; sociable.
usage: He is a gregarious person who loves to party.
She has a gregarious personality.

words.definition: Arrogantly superior and disdainful.
usage: She gave him a haughty look and walked away.
His haughty attitude made him many enemies.

words.definition: Unchanging over time or unable to be changed.
usage: The laws of nature are immutable.
His decision seemed immutable.

words.definition: Having, showing, or done with good judgment or sense.
usage: The judicious use of resources saved the project.
He made a judicious choice.

words.definition: To light or set on fire; to arouse or inspire.
usage: The speech helped kindle hope in the hearts of the people.
She used small sticks to kindle the fire.

words.definition: Using very few words.
usage: His laconic reply suggested he wasn't interested.
She is known for her laconic style of writing.

words.definition: Generous or forgiving, especially toward a rival or less powerful person.
usage: He was magnanimous in victory.
It was a magnanimous gesture on her part.

words.definition: Wicked or criminal.
usage: The nefarious plot was foiled at the last minute.
He was known for his nefarious activities.
